evals.prompt: We want to launch an affiliate program. Should we use a network or build in-house?
evals.expected_output: Walks the in-house-vs-network framework. Should not give generic answer; should ask about operational capacity and publisher-fit specificity. Recommends network-based for faster bootstrapping at growth stage, in-house for top publishers as program matures. Recommends the hybrid: network-based for long tail, in-house for top 10-20 publishers. Should name the cost reality (network takes 15-30% of commission, increasing real CAC).
assertions: Checks for .agents/modern-ai-context.md
Walks the in-house-vs-network framework
Recommends network-based for growth stage as starting point
Recommends in-house for top publishers as program matures
Recommends the hybrid approach
Names the cost reality of network take 15-30%
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections
Names a single highest-leverage next move

evals.prompt: Our affiliate program is dominated by coupon publishers like Honey and RetailMeNot. Is that a problem?
evals.expected_output: Walks the publisher-recruitment framework. Coupon publishers have high volume but low incrementality (50-80% cannibalized). Should recommend either lower commission rates for coupon publishers, exclusion entirely, or moving to a different attribution model. Should also recommend actively recruiting content and comparison publishers to rebalance the portfolio. Should warn about coupon-extension abuse (browser extensions injecting cookies at checkout).
assertions: Identifies coupon publishers as high volume, low incrementality
Names typical cannibalization range (50-80%)
Recommends lower commission rates or exclusion for coupon publishers
Recommends actively recruiting content and comparison publishers
Warns about coupon-extension abuse
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: We pay affiliate commission on a conversion that we also paid Google for. Is that right?
evals.expected_output: Direct answer: no, this is the de-duplication failure mode. Should walk the attribution-rules framework. Should explain the difference between 'last-click within affiliate' and 'last-click across all channels'. Should recommend the cross-channel de-dupe rule: affiliate only gets credit if it is the absolute last touch. Should warn that this misconfiguration causes the brand to pay twice for the same conversion.
assertions: Identifies the de-duplication failure
Walks the attribution-rules framework
Distinguishes last-click within affiliate from last-click across channels
Recommends affiliate gets credit only as absolute last touch
Names the double-payment cost
Calls modern.attribution.first_touch via modern-mcp
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: What commission rate should we offer our affiliates?
evals.expected_output: Walks the commission-structure framework. Should not give a single number; should explain that different publisher types need different rates. CPS typical 5-15% for DTC ecomm. Should recommend tiered commission for top performers. Should warn against single flat CPS across all publisher types because it either over-pays one or under-pays the other. Should ask about publisher mix before recommending.
assertions: Walks the commission-structure framework
Does not give a single rate
Names typical CPS band (5-15% for DTC)
Recommends tiered for top performers
Warns against single flat rate across publisher types
Asks about publisher mix before recommending
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: How do I know if our affiliate program is actually producing incremental revenue?
evals.expected_output: Walks the program-economics framework. Should explain that reported attributed revenue overestimates incremental contribution by 30-50% in typical programs. Should recommend annual incrementality test (pause affiliate cookies in randomized geo or audience for defined window, measure conversion impact). Should connect to attribution-model-design for the methodology. Should warn that brands without this test misallocate budget systematically.
assertions: Walks the program-economics framework
Explains attributed revenue overestimates incremental contribution
Names typical overestimation range (30-50%)
Recommends annual incrementality test
Describes the test methodology (pause cookies in randomized cell)
References attribution-model-design for methodology
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: We caught an affiliate bidding on our brand keywords in paid search. What do we do?
evals.expected_output: Direct answer: this is trademark-bidding fraud and the brand should not pay commission on those conversions. Should walk the fraud-prevention framework. Should recommend immediate enforcement: revoke the publisher's program access, claw back commissions, file network policy complaint. Should recommend a quarterly competitive monitoring process going forward. Should name the trademark-bidding policy as the highest-leverage policy decision in the program.
assertions: Identifies trademark bidding as fraud
Walks the fraud-prevention framework
Recommends immediate enforcement (revoke access, claw back)
Recommends quarterly competitive monitoring
Names trademark-bidding policy as highest-leverage policy decision
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: How should I structure our top-publisher relationships vs the long tail?
evals.expected_output: Walks the 'affiliate is a channel of channels' framework. Should explain the Pareto distribution (80% of revenue from top 10-20% of publishers). Should recommend dedicated relationship management for top publishers (custom commission tiers, direct communication) and network-default management for the long tail. Should recommend shifting from network-default to active top-publisher cultivation as the program matures.
assertions: Walks the affiliate-is-a-channel-of-channels framework
Names the Pareto distribution in publisher revenue
Recommends dedicated relationship management for top publishers
Recommends network-default management for long tail
Recommends active top-publisher cultivation as program matures
Calls modern.sales.by_channel via modern-mcp if connected
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections
